zh.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louw是Wouter I van der Louw和Adeline van Craynem的女兒，於大約1125年出生在荷蘭。她和Willem van Altena至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louw是Wouter I van der Louw和Adeline van Craynem的女儿，于大约1125年出生在荷兰。她和Willem van Altena至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Louw
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from a vernacular short form of the personal name Laurentius (see Lawrence ). This surname is also established in South Africa.
(also De Louw, Van der Louw): occupational name for a tanner, from Middle Dutch louwen ‘to tan’.

variants.name: Lawrence
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Lorens, Laurence, from Latin Laurentius ‘man from Laurentum’, a place in Italy probably named from its laurels or bay trees. The name was borne by a Christian saint who was martyred at Rome in the 3rd century AD ; he enjoyed a considerable cult throughout Europe, with consequent popularity of the personal name (French Laurent, Italian, Spanish Lorenzo, Catalan Llorenç, Portuguese Lourenço, German Laurenz, Polish Wawrzyniec, etc.). In Britain this is a common name from the 12th century, with pet forms such as Law , Low , Lawrie , Laurie , Larry , Larkin , all of which are represented in surnames. There was also a feminine form Laurencia which may have given rise to the English surname. The surname is also borne by Jews among whom it is presumably an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Ashkenazic surnames. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Lorenz , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Slovenian Lavrenčič and Lovrenčič (patronymics from Lavrencij and Lovrenc, equivalents of Lawrence), Polish Wawrzyniak . Compare Larrance , Laurence , Lawerence , Lieurance , and Lowrance .

nameMeanings.name: Alida
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Form of   Adelheid  , influenced by the North German form   Aleit .

en.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louw was born about 1125, in Netherlands as the daughter of Wouter I van der Louw and Adeline van Craynem. She had at least 1 daughter with Willem van Altena.

fr.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louw est née environ 1125, à Pays-Bas, fille de Wouter I van der Louw et Adeline van Craynem. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Willem van Altena.

de.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louw wurde um 1125 in Niederlande, als Tochter von Wouter I van der Louw und Adeline van Craynem geboren. Sie hatte mit Willem van Altena mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louw è nata il circa 1125 nel Paesi Bassi come figlia di Wouter I van der Louw e Adeline van Craynem. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Willem van Altena.

ja.Schoonhoven, ZH Netherlands: オランダズイド-hオランドスチオオンhオベン
ja.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louwは約1125年にオランダでWouter I van der LouwとAdeline van Craynemの娘として生まれた。彼女はWillem van Altenaとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louw(은)는 대략 1125년 네덜란드에서 Wouter I van der Louw(와)과 Adeline van Craynem의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Willem van Altena(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louw nasceu aproximadamente 1125, em Países Baixos filha de Wouter I van der Louw e Adeline van Craynem. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Willem van Altena.

ru.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louw родилась около 1125 г. в местности Нидерланды. Её родителей звали Wouter I van der Louw и Adeline van Craynem. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Willem van Altena.

es.lifeSummary: Alida van der Louw nació aproximadamente 1125, en Países Bajos como hija de Wouter I van der Louw y Adeline van Craynem. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Willem van Altena.
